name: Jerusalem
description: Holy city and capital of ancient Israel; site of Solomon's Temple and Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection.

name: Bethlehem
description: Birthplace of King David and Jesus Christ; known as the City of David.

name: Nazareth
description: Childhood home of Jesus; where the angel Gabriel announced the birth of Jesus to Mary.

name: Babylon
description: Capital of the Babylonian Empire; where the Jews were exiled under Nebuchadnezzar.

name: Rome
description: Capital of the Roman Empire; where Paul was imprisoned and wrote several epistles.

name: Antioch of Syria
description: Where believers were first called Christians; base for Paul's missionary journeys.

name: Corinth
description: Major Greek trading city; Paul established a church here and wrote two epistles to the Corinthians.

name: Damascus
description: One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities; where Paul was converted on the road.

name: Ephesus
description: Major city of Asia Minor; Paul spent three years here establishing the church.

name: Jericho
description: One of the oldest cities in the world; its walls fell before Joshua and the Israelites.

name: Samaria
description: Capital of the northern kingdom of Israel; center of Samaritan worship.

name: Hebron
description: One of the oldest cities; burial place of Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives in the Cave of Machpelah.

name: Capernaum
description: Center of Jesus' Galilean ministry; home of Peter; where many miracles were performed.

name: Bethany
description: Home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus; where Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead.

name: Bethsaida
description: Hometown of Peter, Andrew, and Philip; near where Jesus fed the 5,000.

name: Caesarea Maritima
description: Roman administrative capital of Judea; where Peter baptized Cornelius and Paul was imprisoned.

name: Caesarea Philippi
description: Where Peter confessed Jesus as the Christ; located at the foot of Mount Hermon.

name: Tyre
description: Ancient Phoenician port city; famed for its wealth and commerce; visited by Jesus and Paul.

name: Sidon
description: Ancient Phoenician city; Elijah stayed with the widow of Zarephath near here.

name: Nineveh
description: Capital of the Assyrian Empire; where Jonah was sent to preach repentance.

name: Ur of the Chaldees
description: Birthplace of Abraham; a major Sumerian city in ancient Mesopotamia.

name: Haran
description: Where Abraham's family settled after leaving Ur; where Jacob fled from Esau.

name: Shechem
description: Where Abraham first received God's promise of the land; Jacob purchased land here.

name: Beersheba
description: Southern boundary of Israel ('from Dan to Beersheba'); where Abraham made a covenant with Abimelech.

name: Dan
description: Northern boundary of ancient Israel; where Jeroboam set up a golden calf.

name: Bethel
description: Where Jacob dreamed of a ladder reaching heaven; a major worship site in ancient Israel.

name: Shiloh
description: Location of the Tabernacle for over 300 years before the Temple was built in Jerusalem.

name: Megiddo
description: Strategic fortress city; associated with Armageddon in the book of Revelation.

name: Lachish
description: Second most important city of Judah; besieged by Sennacherib of Assyria.

name: Gaza
description: One of the five Philistine cities; where Samson was imprisoned and died.

name: Ashdod
description: Philistine city where the Ark of the Covenant was taken and the idol Dagon fell.

name: Joppa
description: Ancient port city; where Peter had the vision of the sheet and raised Tabitha from the dead.

name: Thessalonica
description: Major city of Macedonia; Paul founded a church here and wrote two epistles to the Thessalonians.

name: Philippi
description: First European city where Paul preached; where Lydia was converted and Paul was imprisoned.

name: Athens
description: Center of Greek philosophy; where Paul preached at the Areopagus about the unknown God.

name: Crete
description: Mediterranean island where Titus was left to organize churches; Paul's ship passed during his voyage to Rome.

name: Malta
description: Island where Paul was shipwrecked on his voyage to Rome; he healed many there.

name: Cyprus
description: Home island of Barnabas; first stop on Paul's first missionary journey.

name: Patmos
description: Island of exile where the apostle John received the Revelation.

name: Pergamum
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; described as where Satan's throne is.

name: Smyrna
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; praised for faithfulness under persecution.

name: Thyatira
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; home of Lydia, seller of purple cloth.

name: Sardis
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; warned to wake up and strengthen what remains.

name: Philadelphia
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; commended for keeping God's word.

name: Laodicea
description: One of the seven churches of Revelation; rebuked for being lukewarm.

name: Colossae
description: City in Phrygia; Paul wrote the Epistle to the Colossians to the church here.

name: Galatia
description: Region in central Asia Minor; Paul wrote the Epistle to the Galatians to churches here.

name: Derbe
description: City in Lycaonia visited by Paul on his first and second missionary journeys.

name: Lystra
description: City where Paul healed a crippled man and was mistaken for Hermes; Timothy's hometown.

name: Iconium
description: City in Asia Minor visited by Paul; many Jews and Greeks believed.

name: Antioch of Pisidia
description: City where Paul preached in the synagogue on his first missionary journey.

name: Troas
description: Port city where Paul had the vision of a man from Macedonia calling for help.

name: Miletus
description: Port city where Paul gave his farewell address to the Ephesian elders.

name: Puteoli
description: Port near Naples where Paul landed on his voyage to Rome.

name: Syracuse
description: City in Sicily where Paul's ship stopped for three days en route to Rome.

name: Nain
description: Village where Jesus raised a widow's son from the dead.

name: Cana
description: Village where Jesus performed his first miracle, turning water into wine.

name: Emmaus
description: Village where the risen Jesus appeared to two disciples on the road.

name: Sychar
description: Samaritan town near Jacob's well where Jesus spoke with the woman at the well.

name: Dothan
description: Where Joseph's brothers sold him into slavery; where Elisha saw the heavenly army.

name: Ai
description: City near Bethel; Israel's initial defeat here due to Achan's sin.

name: Gilgal
description: First campsite of Israel after crossing the Jordan; twelve memorial stones placed here.

name: Mizpah
description: Meeting place for Israel; where Samuel judged Israel and Saul was chosen as king.

name: Gibeon
description: Where the sun stood still during Joshua's battle; Solomon received wisdom in a dream here.

name: Gezer
description: Strategic Canaanite city; given to Solomon by Pharaoh as a wedding gift.

name: Hazor
description: Largest Canaanite city; burned by Joshua; later fortified by Solomon.

name: Susa
description: Capital of ancient Persia; setting of the book of Esther and Daniel's visions.

name: Persepolis
description: Ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Persian Empire; reflects the power of the empire in Daniel's time.

name: Alexandria
description: Egyptian city with a large Jewish community; home of Apollos; where the Septuagint was translated.

name: Memphis
description: Ancient capital of Egypt; mentioned by the prophets Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Ezekiel.

name: Thebes
description: Ancient Egyptian city called No-Amon in the Bible; mentioned in Nahum's prophecy.

name: Petra
description: Rock-hewn Edomite/Nabataean city; possibly the Sela mentioned in Isaiah and Obadiah.

name: Tadmor
description: Desert city built by Solomon as a trading post in the wilderness.

name: Mount Sinai
description: Where God gave Moses the Ten Commandments and the Law; also known as Mount Horeb.

name: Mount Ararat
description: Where Noah's Ark came to rest after the Great Flood.

name: Mount Carmel
description: Where Elijah challenged the prophets of Baal and called down fire from heaven.

name: Mount of Olives
description: Where Jesus taught, wept over Jerusalem, and ascended to heaven; overlooks the Temple Mount.

name: Mount Zion
description: Hill in Jerusalem symbolizing God's dwelling place; site of David's City and the Upper Room.

name: Mount Moriah
description: Where Abraham was tested to sacrifice Isaac; later the site of Solomon's Temple.

name: Mount Hermon
description: Highest peak in the region; possible site of Jesus' Transfiguration.

name: Mount Nebo
description: Where Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death.

name: Mount Tabor
description: Traditional site of Jesus' Transfiguration; where Deborah and Barak gathered against Sisera.

name: Mount Ebal
description: Mountain of curses; where Joshua built an altar and read the Law to all Israel.

name: Mount Gerizim
description: Mountain of blessings; sacred to the Samaritans as their place of worship.

name: Mount Gilboa
description: Where King Saul and his sons died in battle against the Philistines.

name: Mount Pisgah
description: Peak from which Moses viewed the Promised Land; overlooks the Jordan Valley.

name: Mount Hor
description: Where Aaron died and was buried; located near Petra in Edom.

name: Jordan River
description: Most significant river in the Bible; where Israel crossed into Canaan and where Jesus was baptized by John.

name: Nile River
description: Greatest river of Egypt; turned to blood during the plagues; where baby Moses was found.

name: Euphrates River
description: One of four rivers of Eden; eastern boundary of the Promised Land; mentioned in Revelation.

name: Tigris River
description: One of four rivers of Eden; Daniel received a vision on its banks.

name: Jabbok River
description: Where Jacob wrestled with God and was renamed Israel.

name: Kishon River
description: Where the army of Sisera was swept away; where Elijah slew the prophets of Baal.

name: Arnon River
description: Border between Moab and the Amorites; Israel crossed it during the conquest.

name: Sea of Galilee
description: Freshwater lake central to Jesus' ministry; where Jesus walked on water, calmed the storm, and called his first disciples.

name: Red Sea
description: Sea that God parted for Israel to cross during the Exodus from Egypt.

name: Dead Sea
description: Lowest point on earth; also called the Salt Sea; near Sodom and Gomorrah.

name: Mediterranean Sea
description: Called the Great Sea in the Bible; western boundary of the Promised Land; route for Paul's missionary journeys.

name: Canaan
description: The Promised Land given to Abraham and his descendants; land flowing with milk and honey.

name: Judah
description: Territory of the tribe of Judah; later the southern kingdom after the monarchy split.

name: Samaria Region
description: Region between Galilee and Judea; home of the Samaritans; Jesus traveled through and ministered here.

name: Galilee
description: Northern region where Jesus grew up and conducted much of his ministry.

name: Mesopotamia
description: Land between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers; homeland of Abraham; cradle of civilization.

name: Goshen
description: Region in Egypt where the Israelites settled during Joseph's time and multiplied greatly.

name: Edom
description: Territory of Esau's descendants; south of the Dead Sea; rival of Israel.

name: Moab
description: Region east of the Dead Sea; homeland of Ruth; where Moses died.

name: Ammon
description: Territory of Lot's descendants east of the Jordan; capital was Rabbah (modern Amman).

name: Philistia
description: Coastal region of the five Philistine cities; home of Goliath and enemies of Israel.

name: Bashan
description: Fertile region east of the Sea of Galilee; famed for its oaks and cattle; ruled by King Og.

name: Gilead
description: Mountainous region east of the Jordan; associated with the balm of Gilead; Elijah's homeland.

name: Negev
description: Southern desert of Israel; where Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob traveled and lived.

name: Wilderness of Sinai
description: Desert where Israel wandered for 40 years after the Exodus from Egypt.

name: Wilderness of Judea
description: Barren region between Jerusalem and the Dead Sea; where David hid and Jesus was tempted.

name: Wilderness of Paran
description: Desert where the twelve spies were sent from; where Hagar wandered with Ishmael.

name: Valley of Jezreel
description: Large fertile valley in northern Israel; site of many battles including Gideon's victory over the Midianites.

name: Valley of Elah
description: Where David fought and killed Goliath the Philistine giant.

name: Valley of Hinnom
description: Valley south of Jerusalem associated with child sacrifice to Molech; later called Gehenna.

name: Kidron Valley
description: Valley between Jerusalem and the Mount of Olives; crossed by David fleeing Absalom and by Jesus going to Gethsemane.

name: Valley of Megiddo
description: Strategic valley associated with Armageddon in Revelation; site of King Josiah's death.

name: Valley of Achor
description: Where Achan was punished after the defeat at Ai; prophesied to become a door of hope.

name: Gethsemane
description: Garden at the foot of the Mount of Olives where Jesus prayed in agony before his arrest.

name: Golgotha
modernName: Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem
description: The Place of the Skull where Jesus was crucified.

name: Pool of Bethesda
description: Pool in Jerusalem where Jesus healed a man who had been lame for 38 years.

name: Tarsus
description: Birthplace of the Apostle Paul; a major city of Cilicia.

name: Berea
description: City in Macedonia whose Jews were praised for examining the Scriptures daily.

name: Paphos
description: City in Cyprus where Paul blinded the sorcerer Elymas before the proconsul Sergius Paulus.

name: Salamis
description: City in eastern Cyprus where Paul and Barnabas first preached on the first missionary journey.

name: Perga
description: City in Pamphylia where John Mark left Paul and Barnabas on the first missionary journey.

name: Attalia
description: Port city from which Paul and Barnabas sailed back to Antioch after the first missionary journey.

name: Assos
description: Coastal city where Paul rejoined his companions during his third missionary journey.

name: Ptolemais
description: Coastal city where Paul stopped for a day with believers on his return to Jerusalem.

name: Siloam
description: Pool in Jerusalem where Jesus sent the blind man to wash and receive his sight.
